modules.name: nldi_flowtools.flowtrace
documentation: Generate Raindrop Trace path using NLDI and user-defined point.

documentation: Initialize Flowtrace.

methods.name: nldi_flowtools.flowtrace.Flowtrace.serialize
documentation: Convert returns to GeoJSON to be exported.

methods.name: nldi_flowtools.flowtrace.Flowtrace.run
documentation: Run Flowtrace module.
main functions

Trace downhill from a point to a stream.

Given an longitude and latitude coordinate pair, the flow path will be trace
downhill until it intersects a stream.
This NHD stream can be cut at this point of intersection. With the
third parameter, the user can specify whether they want the downstream or upstream
portion of this stream, or its entire geometry. The flowpath from the query point
to the stream will be returned as the second feature in the output.

Args:
    x (float): Longitude coordinate as a float
    y (float): Latitude coordiante as a float
    direction (str): Either 'down', 'up' or 'none'

Returns:
    A GeoJSON FeatureCollection containing two features; the first is the NHD stream segment
    and the second is the flowtrace path.

modules.name: nldi_flowtools
documentation: Top-level package for pygeoapi plugin: Nldi Flowtools.

modules.name: nldi_flowtools.nldi_flowtools
documentation: Main module.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.nldi_flowtools.splitcatchment
documentation:
Delineate the drainage basin from the input point.

Parameters
----------
lon : float
    The longitude coordinate of the input point in WGS 84 decimal degrees
lat : float
    The latitude coordinate of the input point in WGS 84 decimal degrees
upstream : bool
    Determines whether to return the portion of the drainage basin
    that falls outside of the local catchment. If True, then the
    entire drainage basin is returned. If False, then only the portion
    within the local catchment is returned.

Returns
-------
catchment : geojson
    This is the local NHD catchment that the input point falls within.
    This is also the catchment that gets 'split'.

splitCatchment : geojson
    This polygon can either be a portion or the entire drainage basin for
    the input point, and this depends on if the input point falls on an NHD
    flowline or not. It gets returned if the drainage basin fits only within
    the local catchment, or if the upstream variable is set to False.

drainageBasin : geojson
    This is the entire drainage basin which flows to the input point. It
    will include area outside of the local catchment, and it will only be
    returned if the upstream variable is set to True.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.nldi_flowtools.flowtrace
documentation:
Trace the flowpath to the nearest NHD flowline.

Parameters
----------
lon : float
    The longitude coordinate of the input point in WGS 84 decimal degrees
lat : float
    The latitude coordinate of the input point in WGS 84 decimal degrees
direction : str
    This variable determines which portion of the NHD flowline will be returned.
    'up' returns the portion of the flowline that is upstream from the
    intersection between the raindropPath and the flowline. 'down' returns the
    downstream portion of the flowline from the intersection point. And 'none'
    returns the entire flowline.

Returns
-------
upstreamFlowline : geojson
    The portion of the NHD flowline upstream from the intersection point. This line
    will only be returned in the variable direction is set to 'up'.
downstreamFlowline : geojson
    The portion of the NHD flowline downstream from the intersection point. This line
    will only be returned in the variable direction is set to 'down'.
nhdFlowline : geojson
    This is the entire NHD flowline that the raindropPath intersects with. This line
    will only be returned in the variable direction is set to 'none'.
raindropPath : geojson
    This is the path that water will follow from the input point to the nearest NHD
    flowline. This line will only be returned if the input point does not fall on
    an NHD flowline.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.get_upstream_basin
documentation:
Get the upstream basin geometry.

This is done by subtracting the local catchment geometry from the total basin geometry
(what is returned from the NLDI basin query) and then merging this to the
splitcatchment geometry.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.project_point
documentation: Project point to flow direction raster crs.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.get_flowgrid
documentation:
Get the FDR for the local catchment area.

Use a 90 meter buffer of the local catchment to clip the
NHD Plus v2 flow direction raster.

Args:
    catchment_geom (Polygon or MultiPolygon): Polygon geometry for which to return the
        fdr raster.

Returns:
    A tuple containing a Numpy array and a rasterio Profile.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.get_facgrid
documentation:
Get the FAC for the local catchment area.

Use a 90 meter buffer of the local catchment to clip the
NHD Plus v2 flow accumulation raster.

Args:
    catchment_geom (Polygon or MultiPolygon): Shapely Polygon geometry for which to
        return the fac raster.

Returns:
    A tuple containing a Numpy array and a rasterio Profile.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.split_catchment
documentation: Produce split catchment delienation from X,Y.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.get_row_column
documentation: Given a x,y point and a raster Affine, return the indices of the row and column.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.get_cell_corner
documentation: Given a row column pair, return the coords of the top left corner of a raster cell.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.get_cell_center
documentation: Given an row column pair, return the coordinates of the raster cell center.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.create_cell_polygon
documentation: Given a row, column pair and rasterio affine, return an outline of the raster cell.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.get_on_flowline
documentation:
Determine whether the raster cell 'intersects' the given flowline geometry.

This is not exactly straightforward. The cell needs to both intersect the line geoemtry
and the Flow Accumultion value needs to be above 1100. Only when both of these are true
does the function return a boolean value of 'True'.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.get_downstream_cell
documentation: Return the row, column indices of the next cell downstream given the flow direction.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.trace_downhill
documentation:
Given a starting point, trace down the flow direction grid.

The function returns a list of x,y coords. The first coord pair is the input
coord. The next is the cell center of the cell the point falls in. As the trace
proceeds downstream, each cell center gets added to the coord list.
Once the trace gets to a cell that overlaps the input flowline geometry and the
flow accumulation value is greater that 900, the trace stops. It grabs the closest
point on the flowline and adds it to the coord list.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.get_reach_measure
documentation: Collect NHD Flowline Reach Code and Measure.

functions.name: nldi_flowtools.utils.split_flowline
documentation:
Split the NHD Flowline at the intersection point.

Args:
    intersection_point (Point): The Shapely point at which to split the flowline
    nhd_flowline (LineString): The flowline to split

Returns:
    A tuple containing two shapely LineString. The first is the upstream portion
    and the second is the donwstream portion of the input flowline.
